summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a high‑energy July ’81 show with standout performances of “To Lay Me Down,” “Bird Song,” and an upbeat “Eyes of the World.”
review: The recording is praised as one of the cleanest soundboard sources from July 1981, delivering crisp instrument separation and a punchy guitar tone. Musically the concert shines with a rare Minglewood opener, scorching “Bertha,” a ripping first‑set guitar break, and an especially upbeat “Eyes of the World” jam that many reviewers call a highlight. Overall the setlist flows well, mixing familiar favorites with uncommon choices and keeping the energy high throughout.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (7 of 9) give 4‑star or higher ratings and highlight both excellent soundboard quality and strong musical moments; the few zero‑star entries are statistical notes rather than negative critiques.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers describe the audio as ‘fantastic,’ ‘clear,’ and ‘clean boards’ with no reported drop‑outs or muddiness; the guitar tone is noted as especially crisp.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The setlist is noted as interesting and unique, opening with a rare Minglewood Blues, placing “Music” and “RxR blues” in unexpected spots, and delivering an upbeat second‑set that keeps the momentum high.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for ripping guitar work, a standout break on track 22, and ‘tearing it up’ in the first set.
